risk_if_not_implemented: Without Policy Decision Point (PDP), residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Boundary Protection
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to monitor and control communications at the external network boundary and at key internal boundaries within the network.
compensating_control_1.justification: Boundary Protection (NET-03) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Policy Decision Point (PDP) (NET-04.7) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Data Flow Enforcement – Access Control Lists (ACLs)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to implement and govern Access Control Lists (ACLs) to provide data flow enforcement that explicitly restrict network traffic to only what is authorized.
compensating_control_2.justification: Data Flow Enforcement – Access Control Lists (ACLs) (NET-04) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Policy Decision Point (PDP) (NET-04.7) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
